projectTypeOverlay: Hosted single-owner accounting application candidate with durable financial data, exact four-file fresh-install controller authority, a full ten-file existing-data migration bundle, immutable release and image identity, schema-2 journals, and metadata-backed verified recovery units.
purpose: Private, single-owner EasyFire bookkeeping web application, based on Bigcapital (AGPL-3.0), for recording and reporting financial activity with durable self-hosted storage and recoverable operations.

multiAgent.upgradeProcedure: Run the creating-project-foundations skill from Command Center and request multi-agent mode; do not run a project-local npm command that is not installed here.

deploymentContext.audience: Intended for one owner and administrator. The public hostname is restricted by pre-existing Cloudflare Access plus native application authentication, with public self-registration disabled. The sole owner policy, Tunnel, DNS, token identity, and Running/Automatic cloudflared service are exact; native candidate acceptance still requires owner login after Rehearse.
deploymentContext.networkExposure: Candidate contract: pre-existing Cloudflare Access and Tunnel forward to Envoy on Newsec at 127.0.0.1:80; MariaDB and Redis remain on the internal Compose network. The current controllers verify but never rewrite the edge, whose exact binary, service, process, connector, Tunnel, DNS, Access, and token identity is proven.
deploymentContext.trustedComputeBoundary: Newsec Windows and its Docker runtime are owner-controlled trusted compute. Cloudflare is an external routing and access-control boundary. SSH works; six long-running containers on the older runtime are healthy and its migration container exited 0. Existing durable volumes, legacy journals, releases, backups, and both legacy tasks remain preserved while the separate migration controller binds only candidate-derived resources.
deploymentContext.availabilityTarget: Best-effort personal production service with Compose restart: unless-stopped and one exact daily task that invokes the fifth ScheduledBackup controller stage from the sealed installed controller through canonical Windows PowerShell with the journal-bound deployment-owner SID. Maintenance downtime is acceptable; high availability is not required.
deploymentContext.assuranceTarget: Single-user access control, secret hygiene, exact four-file fresh-install bundles and full ten-file migration bundle, immutable archive and seven-image identity, SYSTEM/Administrators plus journal-bound deployment-owner SID authority, crash-resumable metadata-backed recovery units with isolated restore, schema-2 journals, mandatory Postcheck, complete mount/port containment, TTL-safe Redis continuity, and preserving rollback.

code.remoteUrl: private Forgejo repository jonny-admin/easyfire-bookkeeping (machine-specific endpoint is intentionally not published)
code.recoveryPath: Exact Forgejo main readback defines accepted source and anonymous GitHub readback of the same commit defines AGPL corresponding source. The verified pre-takeover filesystem backup remains the local rollback authority recorded in HANDOFF.md.

sourceOfTruth.runningRuntime: On 2026-07-20, Newsec SSH works; six long-running containers on the older Agent Foundry-origin easyfire-bookkeeping-prod installation are healthy and its migration container exited 0. Existing MariaDB/Redis volumes and pre-schema-2 journals remain durable authority. Both legacy tasks are enabled/Ready with LastTaskResult=1. Access, Tunnel, DNS, token identity, and the Running/Automatic cloudflared service are exact. A verified live-runtime checkpoint and current isolated-restorable MigrationSource backup exist. No candidate rehearsal, native login, or cutover has run.
sourceOfTruth.driftCheck: The candidate cannot use fresh-install Postcheck against incompatible legacy journals. Publish the exact validated recovery patch only to the two EasyFire remotes, build an immutable release and seven pinned images on Newsec, create a new migration Plan, Rehearse, complete owner native login if prompted, run the AcceptAuthentication source recovery drill, and Cutover only after backup, rollback, authentication, and migration proof pass. Then verify task replacement/retirement, runtime/edge health, and a new current backup with isolated restore.

sourceControlPolicy.localCommit: Allowed after validation for durable work when the requested lane is complete.
sourceControlPolicy.createRemote: Requires explicit approval; scaffolding may record a planned URL but does not create or configure it.
sourceControlPolicy.push: Requires explicit approval or a standing project-specific instruction that clearly authorizes publication.
sourceControlPolicy.merge: Requires explicit approval or the project's documented guarded integration workflow.
sourceControlPolicy.forcePush: Prohibited unless the owner approves the exact branch and impact.

guardrails.impactMap: Docs and disclosure: node --test tests/easyfire-release-readiness.test.mjs. Application or dependency changes: frozen install, format, lint, typecheck, build, focused tests, and fake-data E2E. Production scripts or Compose: PowerShell parse, revised static validator, focused controller crash matrix, disposable Docker, schema-2 transitions, migration single-start/timeout, metadata-backed backup/isolated restore, ScheduledBackup, Postcheck, and partial-volume rollback proof. Runtime changes: exact journal-bound Postcheck.

sourceSizePolicy.defaultRule: Warnings are advisory. When a finding blocks under the declared control profile, split it or record an exact path-scoped policy threshold plus the path, lines and severity, disproportionate-split reason, containment proof, and a re-review trigger.

guardrails.contractRegistry: TypeScript application contracts plus Docker Compose restart policies, the production-action schema-2 fresh-install/resume journal, exact deployment bundles, hash-bound environments and images, action-bound volume and port inventory, sealed releases, exact ScheduledBackup authority, crash-resumable recovery units, the caller-bound MigrationSource role, and the executable Plan/Rehearse/AcceptAuthentication/Cutover/Rollback migration journal. Migration authority includes the full ten-file bundle, all seven images, exact mounts/ports, TTL-safe Redis continuity, candidate-only containment, and task changes only at gated Cutover.
guardrails.replayHarness: Synthetic E2E data and a disposable isolated Docker Compose project are required before live production acceptance.
guardrails.stateMutationApproval: Explicit approval is required for deploy, restart, rollback, migration, restore, scheduled-task changes, credentials or integrations, real financial record mutation, and Git publication or integration. The current approved recovery scope authorizes only the named bookkeeping policy/service/tasks plus candidate-only migration work after backup and proof gates pass. The production controller remains verify-only for Cloudflare and fresh-install-only for data.

securityAndPrivacy.securityRequirements: The candidate requires pre-existing Cloudflare Access for one owner identity, native JWT/application authentication, SIGNUP_DISABLED=true, empty signup allowlists, secrets outside Git, no public inbound host application port, exact controller/image/task/edge identity, sealed release identity, and metadata-backed verified backup/restore before completion. Access, Tunnel, DNS, token identity, Newsec SSH, and the Running/Automatic cloudflared service are proven; native candidate acceptance still requires owner login after Rehearse and may pause only for password, MFA, or CAPTCHA entry.
securityAndPrivacy.sensitiveData: Real financial records, identity and contact information, attachments, database credentials, JWT and SMTP secrets, and optional provider credentials may exist only in production-owned storage and secret boundaries.
securityAndPrivacy.dependencyRiskCurrentEvidence: The complete production audit on the release candidate reports 45 advisories: 9 low, 36 moderate, 0 high, and 0 critical. Build-only tools are excluded from production dependencies, patched runtime transitives are lock-enforced, and remaining moderate/low findings stay disclosed for future refreshes.
securityAndPrivacy.remoteSecurity: Exact Forgejo readback defines private accepted-source authority and anonymous GitHub readback of the same commit defines the public AGPL mirror. Secrets, databases, backups, journals, credentials, and user data must never be published.

reproducibility.runtimeVersion: Node.js 22.23.1 from .nvmrc and pnpm 10.34.5 from packageManager; Docker images use immutable release-specific tags or digests.
reproducibility.dependencyManifest: package.json files across the pnpm workspace, pnpm-workspace.yaml, pnpm-lock.yaml, and docker-compose.prod.yml
reproducibility.lockfilePolicy: Commit pnpm-lock.yaml and reject release installs that do not use --frozen-lockfile.

validation.defaultCommand: node --test tests/easyfire-release-readiness.test.mjs && powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/production/validate.ps1 && node scripts/project-foundation-check.mjs && node scripts/source-size-guard.mjs --changed
validation.impactRule: Use the impact map to choose focused checks before broad checks.

runtimeTruth.source: Use sourceOfTruth.runningRuntime for the real running surface. The older runtime is reconciled; the candidate gains runtime authority only through a completed migration journal and post-cutover proof.

ignored: generated build, test, coverage, and restore artifacts
artifactPolicy.committedFixtures: Only safe, minimal fixtures needed for tests.
artifactPolicy.retention: Keep runtime journals off Git as deployment authority. Preserve every observed action-derived durable-volume subset and release directory across rollback. Retain each backup as one recovery unit consisting of its compressed dump, SHA-256 sidecar, and authority-bound metadata, and verify restore separately. Treat every pre-existing project-labeled or historical database/cache volume as migration authority, never as a fresh-install target. Summarize or rotate bulky diagnostics.
